prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic devotional oil painting video clip, controlled pacing with deliberate cuts and extended holds.

Classical devotional oil painting realism. Warm light falls across an embrace and a scarred hand on a shoulder while the background recedes into soft shadow, an open doorway of light beyond; visible brushwork, craquelure, aged varnish throughout.

Scene contains:
The risen Christ, robed in white, draws a kneeling man up into an embrace, his scarred hand resting on the man's shoulder, both faces close in tender recognition, subtle background vignettes fading into shadow suggesting the embrace, a wounded hand on a shoulder, a gathered welcoming family, and an open doorway of light. The scene is a painted tableau — a frozen moment, not an action. Nothing moves; this is a still painting.

Scene:
Start on full composition — the risen Christ embracing the brother.

Cut to mid framing — the two figures close in the embrace.

Cut to close-up — the two faces close in recognition.

Cut to insert — the scarred hand on the man's shoulder.

Cut to insert — the man's upturned face.

Cut to upper framing — the open doorway of light behind.

Return to full composition.

End hold on the unashamed embrace.

Micro-motion:
- minimal

Camera:
- crop-based reframing only
- no invented movement

Emotion:
Tender, unashamed welcome.

No added elements.

10.0s

9:16
negative_prompt: no camera invention, no zoom invented, no pan invented, no push-in invented, no dolly, no tracking, no rotation, no animated subject motion, no figures moving, no morphing, no scene morphing, no facial morphing, no glow added, no halo added, no light rays added beyond what is painted, no atmospheric haze, no scene enhancement, no divine effects added, no smooth transitions, no motion blur, no reinterpretation, no new elements, no text, no watermarks, no extra limbs, no flickering, no warping, no animation, no action, no subject performs any task, no body motion, no limbs moving, the painting is fixed and frozen, only the camera reframes within the still image

beats.description: Start on full composition — risen Christ embracing the brother

Return to full composition, end hold on the unashamed embrace
